item.title: Trump Is Remaking the World Map. What Could Go Wrong?
item.description: A system of fuzzy borders, in which powerful states treat territory as negotiable and sovereignty as conditional, is not a viable alternative to the liberal world order.

item.title: Writing Is Fundamental to How We Think
item.description: A.I. can be a crutch that hurts our ability to think creatively.

item.title: The Prison System Doesn’t Encourage Change. Time Does.
item.description: A formerly incarcerated writer reflects on how the prison system didn’t foster change, but befriending time did.

item.title: Prison and Time
item.description: A formerly incarcerated writer reflects on how the prison system didn’t foster change, but befriending time did.

item.title: Every New Cease-Fire in Lebanon Brings Hope — Until It Doesn’t
item.description: In Lebanon, each new cease-fire is met with blind optimism — as if it hails the end of a conflict instead of what it actually is: an admission ticket to the next war.

item.title: Mets Fans Are Struggling. Be Gentle.
item.description: The Mets keep finding ways to disappoint us. And we still keep finding ways to never give up hope.

item.title: What Ken Paxton’s Win Means for James Talarico
item.description: The Texas senate race will come down to the Democratic candidate’s strengths — and the Republican’s weaknesses.

item.title: Yuval Noah Harari on Donald Trump’s Core Delusion
item.description: The best-selling author of “Sapiens” and “Nexus” discusses the core delusions deranging our politics.

item.title: It’s Crow, Mr. Trump, Not Lobster
item.description: Whatever deal is reached between the United States and Iran, Trump will have to make a big concession: A murderous regime remains intact.

item.title: Deal or No Deal With Iran
item.description: A deal to end the current blockade is merely an enticement for the next blockade and the one after that.

item.title: Yuval Noah Harari on Donald Trump’s Core Delusion
item.description: The best-selling author of “Sapiens” and “Nexus” discusses the core delusions deranging our politics.

item.title: Outrage Over Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund
item.description: Readers praise the opposition from some Republicans, call the fund a legal “travesty” and discuss the culture of corruption. Also: Living with dementia.

item.title: As a Doctor, I Can Understand the Allure of ChatGPT
item.description: Even doctors can learn something from A.I.

item.title: Pope Leo’s Encyclical on A.I. Is Disappointingly Mild
item.description: “Magnifica Humanitas” was measured and cautious.

item.title: The Hard Truth My Party Needs to Face
item.description: We must use our leverage to end Israel’s occupation and establish two states with full political and legal rights for all.

item.title: Progressives Are Listening to the Wrong People on A.I.
item.description: An obsession with the most doomer prospects of A.I. is blinding progressives from thinking about serious, realistic policies.

item.title: What Your Kid’s Report Card Isn’t Telling You
item.description: Grades are going up and test scores are going down.

item.title: The Simple Answer to Taxing the Rich Is the Best Answer
item.description: Forget complex schemes. There is an easier way to get revenue from the wealthy.

item.title: Iran Is Trolling Us and We’re Not Doing Anything About It
item.description: This is a novel form of propaganda.

item.title: The Ghosts of France’s Slave Trade Will Be Silenced No More
item.description: Three centuries late and not a moment too soon.

item.title: How Democrats Can Win, According to This Nobel Economist
item.description: An expert in human behavior explains how Democrats can change their primary process to improve their chances in 2028.

item.title: The Medical System Abandons Women When They Are Most Vulnerable
item.description: One year after my daughter’s birth, I’m still experiencing health complications.

item.title: What A.I. Philanthropists Can Learn From the Gilded Age
item.description: Leave a physical legacy to future generations, not just a record of programs and disbursements.

item.title: Stop Whining About Guardrails. Become One Yourself.
item.description: Let’s put the sovereign back Into popular sovereignty.

item.title: Forget Ozempic. Brain Health Is the New Wellness Frontier.
item.description: We’ve never been so mindful of our minds

item.title: How to Be Old
item.description: My advice for how to keep living.

item.title: Dan Pfeiffer Thinks Two Democrats Could Upend American Politics
item.description: Why 2026 might tell us a lot about 2028.

item.title: Falling Test Scores: Reasons and Fixes
item.description: Readers discuss data showing lower reading and math scores. Also: The yuppie factor.

item.title: My Wife Fought Corruption in El Salvador. Then She Vanished Into Its Prisons.
item.description: The cost of Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on crime is too high.

item.title: The Moms Delivered for Trump. Now He’s Scamming Them.
item.description: The moms are not happy.

item.title: Why Americans Are Afraid of Their Neighbors
item.description: ‘Stranger danger’ was always a misleading idea, and it’s bad for our children.

item.title: Memories of Growing Up on Long Island
item.description: A guest essayist wrote that Long Island produced “more than its share of villainous, dead-eyed jerks.” Readers offer their perspectives. Also: Honor at Stanford.

item.title: I Need an Escape From Travel
item.description: If I cannot get an upgrade — and I cannot, as I have no status, in any aspect of my life — I would like us to suffer more equally when we travel.

item.title: The 15-Year-Old Keeping War Memories Alive
item.description: Luke Morrison may be the youngest person keeping alive an age-old tradition: to process war through the memories and mementos of those who experienced it.

item.title: Now Is Not the Time to Play Political Make-Believe
item.description: Voters want to dictate which offices their preferred politicians hold, what positions they take, and even where and how they live. It is fantasy football: political edition.

item.title: Graduating Into A.I. Pessimism
item.description: Backlash to artificial intelligence is here, and digital natives are leading the charge. The Opinion columnist Michelle Goldberg argues that the A.I. pessimism in America stems from a lack of responsible regulation.

item.title: Inside the $1.8 Billion MAGA Payout Fund
item.description: Americans should be cleareyed about what President Trump is doing: He is taking $1.8 billion worth of taxpayer money and rewarding loyalists willing to defy the law and commit violence. The Times editorial board explains in this video.

item.title: Firing Cancer Screening Experts Will Not Make Us Healthy Again
item.description: The future of health advice is uncertain.

item.title: Has Trump Gone Full ‘Mob Boss’?
item.description: The president’s $1.8 billion slush fund is causing further cracks in the Republican Party.

item.title: ‘We Should All Apply’ to Trump’s Slush Fund
item.description: Trump’s proposed $1.8 billion compensation fund tied to Jan. 6 is drawing scrutiny. On “The Opinions,” David French explains why he sees a double standard between how rioters and ordinary Americans would be treated under the system.

item.title: Has Trump Gone Full ‘Mob Boss’?
item.description: The president’s $1.8 billion slush fund is causing further cracks in the Republican Party.

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Finale Didn’t Mean What It Seemed To
item.description: You’re going to miss corporate media.

item.title: There’s a Way to Stop Trump’s I.R.S. Slush Fund
item.description: There should be no payouts to Jan. 6 rioters.

item.title: Washington Is Still Trying to Rewrite Cuban History
item.description: How can Cubans move forward if they aren’t ready to reckon with their past?

item.title: The Agony Around the Democrats’ Mysterious, Ridiculous Autopsy
item.description: After this fiasco, Ken Martin, the chair of the D.N.C., should be replaced.

item.title: Court Cases About Money and Speech in Politics
item.description: Readers discuss court cases about campaign finance. Also: Computer science and the humanities.

item.title: Pope Leo Chooses Social Justice Over Pelvic Theology
item.description: The document will address A.I. and its impact on humanity. He’s also staying the course on Catholic social teaching.

item.title: A.I. Is a Job Creator
item.description: A.I. will create more jobs than it will kill.

item.title: Your Questions About Nicholas Kristof’s Column on Palestinians and Sexual Assault
item.description: The Times offers answers to some of the pressing questions raised.
